* Refactor Firestore rules and enhance race management features

- Updated Firestore rules to allow group admins to manage active member roles.
- Added circuitTimezone property to various request and response types for race management.
- Implemented updateRaceCircuitTimezone function to allow admins to set race timezones.
- Enhanced AdminPage and PicksPage to support circuit timezone selection and display.
- Introduced F1_CIRCUIT_TIMEZONES for user-friendly timezone selection in the Admin interface.

* Enhance AdminPage, DashboardPage, LeaderboardPage, and PicksPage with various improvements

- Added comments to clarify the use of state synchronization in AdminPage and PicksPage.
- Simplified boolean logic for notification preferences in DashboardPage.
- Refactored leaderboard data handling in LeaderboardPage for better readability and performance.
- Updated PicksPage to handle optional race ID and existing picks more robustly.

* Add 'requestedAt' field to Firestore rules for enhanced data tracking

* Enhance Firebase deployment workflow and AdminPage race synchronization

- Added a check to ensure the Firestore rules file exists before deployment in the GitHub Actions workflow.
- Updated the runRaceSync function to return an 'alreadySynced' flag, indicating if a race has already been synced.
- Introduced a new RaceSyncResponse type in AdminPage to handle race synchronization responses.
- Implemented race synchronization functionality in AdminPage, including user notifications for sync status.

* Implement team theme selection and enhance AdminPage functionality

- Replaced the theme toggle button with a dropdown for selecting team themes in ThemeToggle component.
- Introduced a new TeamThemeId type and TEAM_THEMES constant for managing theme options.
- Updated AdminPage to include selectedSeasonYear in the state and added live roster counts fetching logic.
- Enhanced various components with improved styling and accessibility features.
- Added CSS variables for new team themes to support dark mode and improve UI consistency.

* Add messaging functionality and update Firestore rules

- Introduced a new MessagesPage for group messaging, allowing users to send and receive messages with @-mention support.
- Updated Firestore rules to manage message access and permissions for group members.
- Added a function to post system messages to groups upon leaderboard synchronization.
- Enhanced routing and app layout to include the new MessagesPage.

* Remove unused LiveRosterCounts type after merge

Made-with: Cursor

* Add live roster counts fetching functionality in AdminPage

- Introduced a new type LiveRosterCounts to track the number of drivers and constructors.
- Implemented fetchLiveRosterCounts function to retrieve and return the counts from Firestore.
- Added a liveRosterQuery to manage the state of live roster counts in AdminPage.

* Refactor LiveRosterCounts type in AdminPage

- Removed the standalone LiveRosterCounts type and integrated its structure directly into the fetchLiveRosterCounts function's return type.
- Updated the fetchLiveRosterCounts function to streamline the fetching of driver and constructor counts from Firestore.

* fix: use inline return type for fetchLiveRosterCounts to resolve TS2304

Made-with: Cursor

commit.message:
Replace XNU Image Fuzzer copy with git submodule

Remove the manually-copied XNU Image Fuzzer directory and replace it with a git
submodule pointing to xsscx/xnuimagefuzzer. This eliminates code drift between
the two repos — xnuimagefuzzer is now the single source of truth for fuzzer code.

Changes:
- .gitmodules: Add XNU Image Fuzzer submodule (xsscx/xnuimagefuzzer)
- All 6 CI workflows: Add submodules: recursive to 21 checkout steps
- copilot-instructions.md: Update title, add clone instructions, document
  submodule architecture and VideoToolbox/Generator subprojects

The submodule path 'XNU Image Fuzzer' preserves existing Xcode workspace
references in XNU Image Tools.xcodeproj without project file modifications.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix mb --ignore-existing failing on first run with S3-compatible services (#31)

* Initial plan

* fix: handle bucket_exists errors for S3-compatible services in mb --ignore-existing

Two fixes for `mb --with-versioning --ignore-existing` failing on first run:

1. S3 client: Check HTTP 404 status code in bucket_exists() in addition
   to string matching, since some S3-compatible services return different
   error formats that don't contain "NotFound" or "NoSuchBucket".

2. mb command: When --ignore-existing is set and bucket_exists() returns
   an error, proceed to try creating the bucket instead of failing
   immediately. The create_bucket call will surface any real errors.

Co-authored-by: overtrue <1472352+overtrue@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: check HTTP 404 status before string formatting in bucket_exists()

commit.message:
perf: pool WAL segment read handles via SafeFileHandle + RandomAccess (#1261)

#1240: Each record read previously opened and closed a new FileStream.
Under concurrent load (e.g., 50 users × queries touching 100 records
each = 5000 file opens), this created excessive OS overhead and risked
exhausting the file descriptor limit.

Fix:
- Cache SafeFileHandle per segment in ConcurrentDictionary
- Replace FileStream.Seek/Read with RandomAccess.Read (thread-safe,
  no locking needed — multiple threads can read different offsets
  from the same handle concurrently)
- Evict handles before segment deletion/replacement in compaction
- Dispose all cached handles in WalStore.Dispose()

Result: A query touching 1000 records from the same segment now
opens 1 file handle instead of 1000.

Co-authored-by: WillEastbury <WillEastbury@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Masked fld file (#2315)

Hi,

Here I add very simple code that allows to write out portions of the
domain in an fld_file.

The way I do now in the user module is:
```fortran
    call output%write_masked(temp, my_point_zone%mask, time%t)
```

The problem here is that the output must be of type `fld_file_t`. I
can't initialize it as `file_t` because that one does not have the
`write_masked` procedure. So for this I ask your help on how you think
we should make it as seamless as possible (if you have ideas).

This would work if one uses the "full_element" option for the point
zones, so eventually one could have a general option that allows to
automatically write out whichever part of the domain is needed.

I duplicated a lot of code on purpose. I felt that I would end up with a
lot of extra optional arguments and a lot of IF statements if I did not
do that. Let me know if you think I should change that too.

Again, I would have prefered that fld_file_t does not handle this type
of stuff. It is just a file and should not care about masks, logic, etc.
But the other option of reducing inputs to contiguous memory and all
that was much more intrusive.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve Alembic multiple heads and fix CORS configuration (#60)

## Problems

Two bugs blocking the testing environment:

### 1. Alembic multiple head revisions
`alembic upgrade head` (run on container startup) failed with:
> Multiple head revisions are present for given argument 'head'

The migration DAG had two independent heads:
- `abbdcb5d6cb9` — existing merge of the telegram-bot and cities
branches
- `a1b2c3d4e5f6` — login_codes / password_reset_tokens branch that was
never merged

### 2. CORS error on all browser requests
`allow_origins=["*"]` combined with `allow_credentials=True` is
**invalid per the CORS spec**. Browsers reject this combination, causing
every credentialed request from the frontend to fail.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `alembic/versions/f1e2d3c4b5a6_merge_all_heads.py` | New merge
migration — `down_revision = ('abbdcb5d6cb9', 'a1b2c3d4e5f6')`, empty
body |
| `app/main.py` | Read allowed origins from `CORS_ORIGINS` env var
(comma-separated) instead of `"*"` |
| `docker-stack.yml` | Pass `CORS_ORIGINS` from the server `.env` into
the backend container |

## Action required
Set the `CORS_ORIGINS` GitHub secret in each environment:
- **testing**: `CORS_ORIGINS=https://alumap.tst.escalopa.com`
- **production**: `CORS_ORIGINS=https://alumap-prod.escalopa.com`

The companion infra PR wires this secret through the Ansible
provisioning pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Repo Assist] Fix docs: switch to fsdocs-tool 21.0.0 to resolve namespace substitution (fixes #269) (#270)

* Fix docs: switch to fsdocs-tool 21, fix namespace substitution

- Replace fsharp.formatting.commandtool 11.4.3 with fsdocs-tool 21.0.0
  The old tool used MSBuild v15.1.0.0 which can't load the
  SetRidAgnosticValueForProjects task from .NET SDK 8.x, causing project
  cracking to fail silently. fsdocs-tool 21 uses Ionide.ProjInfo

- Add FsDocsLicenseLink and FsDocsReleaseNotesLink to project file so

Closes #269

commit.message:
fix: restrict CORS origins using CORS_ORIGINS env var (#61)

## Problem
The backend had `allow_origins=["*"]` hardcoded, allowing all origins
regardless of environment.

## Changes
- Read `CORS_ORIGINS` environment variable (comma-separated list of
allowed origins)
- Falls back to `"*"` in development (`DEV`/`TEST`) when not set
- In production the value is injected by the infra Terraform config

## Notes
Android native HTTP clients (Flutter/Dart `http`) do not send an
`Origin` header and bypass CORS entirely — no whitelisting needed for
the Android app.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add agent and plugin spec conformance validation (#279)

* Add agent and plugin spec conformance validation

Extend the validation pipeline to check .agent.md files and plugin.json
files alongside skills. Previously only SKILL.md files were validated.

Agent validation (AgentProfiler):
- Frontmatter required (error)
- Name and description required (error)
- Name format: same rules as skills (lowercase alphanum + hyphens, 64 max)
- Name must match filename minus .agent.md suffix
- Description max 1024 chars
- Body max 500 lines

Plugin validation (PluginValidator):
- Name required, must match directory name
- Version and description required
- Description max 1024 chars
- Skills path required and must exist
- Agents path warned if specified but missing

Discovery (SkillDiscovery):
- DiscoverAgents() walks up from skill paths to plugin roots,
  scans agents/ directories for .agent.md files
- DiscoverPlugins() finds plugin.json from skill paths

Integration (ValidateCommand):
- Runs after skill discovery, before evaluation
- Errors block with exit code 1 (same as spec_conformance_failure)

All 4 existing agents and 2 plugins pass cleanly.
25 new tests (13 agent, 12 plugin).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Code review fixes: spec comments, ValidateNameFormat, kind-specific messages

- Refactor ValidateName into ValidateNameFormat (format-only) + ValidateName
  (format + directory match) so agent/plugin validation doesn't abuse the
  skill directory-match check by passing name twice.
- Warning messages now say 'Agent name' or 'Plugin name' instead of
  'Skill name' when called from AgentProfiler/PluginValidator.
- Add spec URL comments to AgentProfiler and PluginValidator referencing
  agentskills.io/specification, VS Code agent-plugins docs, and Claude Code
  plugins-reference.
- Add missing agent name edge-case tests: leading/trailing hyphen,
  consecutive hyphens, and message-prefix assertions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused agents path from plugins that have no agents

The dotnet, dotnet-data, and dotnet-upgrade plugins declared an agents
path but had no agents/ directory, producing validation warnings. Remove
the field; it can be added back when agents are actually created.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Promote agent/plugin name validation from warnings to errors

The agentskills.io spec uses 'Must' for all name constraints (length,
character set, no leading/trailing hyphens, no consecutive hyphens).
For a publishing validator these should block, not just warn. Agent
filename mismatch is also promoted since the IDE won't discover the
agent if the name doesn't match.

Skill name validation stays as warnings (lenient loading, not part
of this PR).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Fix FrontmatterRegex: remove RegexOptions.Multiline so a horizontal
  rule (---) in the body is not mistaken for frontmatter. Detection and
  stripping now both anchor to start of string.
- Use filename as fallback identifier in AgentProfile when agent.Name
  is empty, so error output shows [agent:foo.agent.md] not [agent:].
- Add TryGetSafeSubdirectory to PluginValidator: rejects absolute paths
  and parent-directory traversal in skills/agents path fields. Used by
  both PluginValidator and SkillDiscovery.DiscoverAgents.
- Use DirectoryName as fallback for plugin result name when Name is
  empty, so error output shows [plugin:my-dir] not [plugin:].
- Add tests for path traversal rejection and name fallback behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Detect orphaned test directories with no matching plugin/skill

Add FindOrphanedTestDirectories to SkillDiscovery: checks that every
directory under tests/{plugin}/{skill}/ has a corresponding directory
under plugins/{plugin}/skills/{skill}/. Plugin-level orphans (tests/
directory with no matching plugins/ directory) are also flagged.

Integrated into ValidateCommand after plugin/agent validation so it
runs on every validation pass. Errors are blocking (exit 1).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR #279 review feedback

- Use case-sensitive (Ordinal) comparison for agent filename matching
  since agent names are required to be lowercase by spec.
- Replace OrdinalIgnoreCase path-under-root check in TryGetSafeSubdirectory
  with Path.GetRelativePath + '..' prefix check for correct behavior on
  case-sensitive filesystems.
- Let ParsePluginJson throw JsonException on malformed JSON instead of
  silently returning null. ValidateCommand catches it as a blocking error.
- Add test for malformed plugin.json.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Increase eval timeouts for dump-collect and dotnet-trace-collect to 120s

The with-skill runs for these skills were hitting the 60s scenario timeout
in CI, causing the agent to be killed before producing output. Other skills
in the same plugin already use 120s. The 30s decline/negative-test scenario
is left unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix fixture paths in clr-activation-debugging and analyzing-dotnet-performance evals

The source paths referenced tests/dotnet/ but the fixtures live under
tests/dotnet-diag/. This caused file-not-found exceptions at runtime,
which set hasRejections and caused unconditional exit 1 (bypassing
--verdict-warn-only).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Surface execution errors in validation summary

Previously, skills that threw exceptions (e.g. missing fixture files)
were silently excluded from the verdict count. The summary showed
'2/4 skills passed' with no indication that 2 other skills crashed
entirely. Now the summary shows '2/6 skills passed (2 rejected due
to execution errors)' and lists the specific error messages near
the summary rather than buried in the middle of the log.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce parallelism for infra-change evaluation runs

When infrastructure changes trigger evaluation of all plugins
simultaneously, the high default parallelism (5x5x5) causes API
rate limits (429s) and scenario timeouts from contention. Reduce
to 2x3x3 for infra-change runs only; regular single-plugin PR
evaluations keep the default 5x5x5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

// Old (with Params)

commit.message:
feat(feature): flatten sub-commands for Copilot CLI

Replace the commands and skills symlinks in plugins-copilot/feature/
with real directories containing Copilot-specific files.

Previously the single commands/feature.md file caused Copilot CLI to
expose the command as /feature:feature. Now each sub-command is its
own file, yielding /feature:start, /feature:end, /feature:checkpoint,
/feature:status, /feature:catchup, /feature:handoff, /feature:resume.

Also replace the skills symlink with real SKILL.md files so that:
- ${COPILOT_PLUGIN_ROOT} is used instead of ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}
- feature-checkpoint and feature-status invoke /feature:checkpoint
  and /feature:status (not the old sub-command form)

The scripts/ symlink and canonical plugins/feature/ are unchanged.
